content.text:
All 4 tests pass, with 5 remaining as todo.

---

Green Phase Complete:

**Implementation**: Implemented the full Game of Life next-generation logic in `nextGeneration()` using a sparse representation. The function builds a `Set` of live cell keys for O(1) lookup, counts neighbors for all cells adjacent to live cells using a `Map`, then applies the standard rules: a live cell with 2 or 3 neighbors survives; a dead cell with exactly 3 neighbors is born.

**Result**: All 4 tests pass (4 passed, 5 todo).

**Approach**: This is the minimal implementation required to make the blinker test pass. The blinker test exercises survival (2 neighbors), underpopulation (1 neighbor causing death), and reproduction (dead cells with 3 neighbors becoming alive) -- all three core Game of Life rules. A simpler hardcoded approach would break the previously passing tests, so the general algorithm was necessary.

**File modified**: `/home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-11_03-56-11_game-of-life-example-mapping_v4.2-conservative_opus-4-6-portkey/src/game-of-life.ts`
